title: General Expectant/New Parent Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > General Expectant/New Parent Assistance 26 Matching Service Providers 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH FAMILY CONNECTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A nurse visiting program that offers up to four scheduled nurse visits to support families with a newborn. Provides overall health checks, help with feeding and bathing, and support for birthing parents with postpartum depression. Nurses also help connect families with community resources to promote parents’ health and children’s well-being. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Birth, adoptive, or resource families with a newborn 0-12 weeks Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 760-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 2665 Villa Creek Drive Farmers Branch, TX 75234 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (469) 343-4081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS PORTLAND BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 1340 SW Bertha Boulevard Suite 201 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 894-7026 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CROOK COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH FAMILY CONNECTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A nurse visiting program that offers up to four scheduled nurse visits to support families with a newborn. Expectant parents can register for prenatal classes. Also directs families to parent-child and parenting classes available through local agencies. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW LM-132 Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Expectant families and families with children ages 0-5 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 917-4884 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH NURSE FAMILY PARTNERSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that supports pregnant people from early pregnancy through their child’s second birthday, pairing new parents with a specially trained nurse for support, advice and resource connection. Nurses provide health and developmental screenings to identify potential problems with growth, hearing, social-emotional development, oral health, and other issues. Offers breastfeeding support, parenting support, and general parenting education. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Pregnant people with a household income below 185% FPL, must enroll prior to the 29th week of pregnancy Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-11:30am and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 774-8203 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH FAMILY CONNECTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A nurse visiting program that offers up to four scheduled nurse visits to support families with a newborn.
